title: No Lessons Learned

jokes.text: Just more government interference. Next thing you know, they won't let me have an abortion on the plane.
jokes.setup: Flight attendant asks Larry to fasten seatbelt
jokes.punchline: Larry escalates minor seatbelt request to absurd abortion scenario
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry escalation from reasonable request to absurd government overreach. Perfect character voice and unexpected comparison.

jokes.text: I'm not really interested in your opinion. I don't consider you a person.
jokes.setup: Leon offers to watch Seinfeld and give his opinion
jokes.punchline: Larry dismisses Leon entirely as a non-person
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest Larry dismissal delivered with characteristic bluntness. High quotability factor.

jokes.text: Because I'd like to know if there's a squealer on this plane. I'd like to know who it is. I'm not comfortable flying with a squealer.
jokes.setup: Flight attendant won't reveal who reported Larry's phone
jokes.punchline: Larry treats airplane safety like a prison yard snitching situation
jokes.explanation: Larry's paranoia about being reported creates prison movie atmosphere on airplane.

jokes.text: Sir, you're not comfortable with a squealer yet you're asking me to squeal? That's a different kind of squealing.
jokes.setup: Larry wants to know who squealed on him
jokes.punchline: Flight attendant points out Larry's hypocrisy with wordplay
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'squealing' but flight attendant character isn't well established.

jokes.text: Why would I draw attention when I got my own fucking phone on? His phone's on? Check it out.
jokes.setup: Larry accuses Leon of squealing on him
jokes.punchline: Leon reveals his own phone is on, then immediately squeals on Larry
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and payoff with Leon's logic backfiring immediately. Good escalation structure.

jokes.text: You never told me it was a show about weekly ass. Jerry just constantly got ass every week. This ain't a fucking TV show. This is more of a fuck documentary.
jokes.setup: Leon watched Seinfeld episodes on the plane
jokes.punchline: Leon's crude analysis that Seinfeld is actually a sex documentary
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Leon perspective on Seinfeld through his unique worldview. 'Fuck documentary' is perfect Leon phrasing.

jokes.text: You need extra dicks. Like you got your own dick, but then you gotta have a backup dick. A dick should be interchangeable. Yeah. Like a pair of glasses.
jokes.setup: Leon wondering how Jerry had so much sex
jokes.punchline: Leon's practical solution of interchangeable backup dicks like glasses
jokes.explanation: Peak Leon absurdist logic. The glasses comparison elevates it from crude to brilliantly ridiculous.

jokes.text: Oh, I'mma take my other dick out my back pocket.
jokes.setup: Continuing the backup dick theory
jokes.punchline: Leon acts out retrieving backup dick from back pocket
jokes.explanation: Natural extension of the backup dick bit with physical comedy element.

jokes.text: We're trying to get in! She's speeding up! We wanna get off the highway! Are you kidding me? What is with this woman? Won't let us in!
jokes.setup: Larry and Leon trying to merge lanes
jokes.punchline: Driver actively preventing them from merging, causing road rage
jokes.explanation: Standard road rage setup for later payoff. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Most popular White man in America right now. You don't have to buy a pack of Kools for the rest of your life.
jokes.setup: Larry being recognized as the voting rights defendant
jokes.punchline: His legal troubles have made him popular with Black community
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about Larry's unlikely hero status. Kools reference adds specificity.

jokes.text: I'm 76 years old and I have never learned a lesson in my entire life.
jokes.setup: Mother trying to teach her child to apologize
jokes.punchline: Larry offers his own life as a cautionary example
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry self-awareness and life philosophy in one line. Highly quotable episode theme statement.

jokes.text: That's who we don't wanna be like, okay? Come on. Let's go.
jokes.setup: Larry's declaration about never learning lessons
jokes.punchline: Mother uses Larry as negative example for her child
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat that completes the lesson sequence. Mother's practicality is funny.

jokes.text: We don't wanna hear that. Why can't you-- 'Cause it's a picture of you, uh... No, no! You don't have to picture me. Picture her!
jokes.setup: Richard sharing TMI about his sex life with Cynthia
jokes.punchline: Richard's desperate attempt to redirect the mental imagery
jokes.explanation: Standard TMI cringe comedy. Richard's logic fails spectacularly but predictably.

jokes.text: I'd rather picture my parents naked than you naked.
jokes.setup: Discussion about not wanting to picture Richard having sex
jokes.punchline: Larry escalates to most disturbing comparison possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry escalation to worst possible scenario. Economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: You think you're suicide material? Yeah, yeah. Absolutely.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Cynthia's alleged suicide attempt over Richard
jokes.punchline: Larry questions if Richard is worth killing yourself over; Richard confidently affirms
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with perfect Larry skepticism and Richard's oblivious confidence.

jokes.text: What-- I don't think I-- Doesn't sound like me. Do you have a blue Mercedes? I do. But it wasn't me. I wasn't even on I-85 today.
jokes.setup: Larry confronts Cynthia about not letting them merge
jokes.punchline: Despite having the exact car and location, Cynthia denies it was her
jokes.explanation: Satisfying callback but Cynthia's denial is too obvious to be particularly funny.

jokes.text: Don't say it in front of people. I told you that in confidence. It is. Telling people what sexual positions you like, that would be personal.
jokes.setup: Larry mentions Cheryl doesn't like Mexican food in front of others
jokes.punchline: Cheryl treats food preference like intimate secret, Larry escalates comparison
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing different levels of personal information. Larry's comparison is appropriately inappropriate.

jokes.text: When I was 19 years old, I was in a bar. I had a drink. And I looked at the bartender and I said to him, 'Hey, Captain, I'll have another,' and he gave me like the dirtiest look. And I've never called anybody captain since.
jokes.setup: Susie challenges Larry about never learning lessons
jokes.punchline: Larry's one 'learned lesson' is incredibly trivial and specific
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback with absurdly specific and meaningless 'lesson.' Classic Larry logic.

jokes.text: Not captain, but I did call a motherfucker chief, and then realized he was a real Indian and shit.
jokes.setup: Larry's captain story
jokes.punchline: Leon's parallel story with more serious cultural implications
jokes.explanation: Leon's version escalates the stakes from Larry's minor embarrassment to actual cultural offense.

jokes.text: Well, you know, baby, I'm glad you're enjoying it, but that's one recipe that they can't pull out of me. Even for you, baby, I don't do. Mic drop!
jokes.setup: Susie begging for the salad dressing recipe
jokes.punchline: Auntie Rae's absolute refusal with theatrical mic drop gesture
jokes.explanation: Auntie Rae's theatrical refusal sets up the later recipe theft. Good character moment.

jokes.text: Who here could see themselves stealing a loaf of bread in order to feed a hungry stranger? I ask they're all dismissed for cause.
jokes.setup: Prosecutor asks jury about moral flexibility
jokes.punchline: Larry wants to dismiss all empathetic jurors
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Larry wants to eliminate jurors who might understand his charitable act.

jokes.text: That guy's got a string tie him. Let's get rid of him. No liberal would be caught dead in a string tie.
jokes.setup: Larry judging potential jurors
jokes.punchline: Larry's superficial profiling based on string tie
jokes.explanation: Larry's shallow political profiling. String tie observation is oddly specific and probably accurate.

jokes.text: You see that comb over? He's a self-loathing bald man! He hates himself and he hates other bald men.
jokes.setup: Larry continuing to profile jurors
jokes.punchline: Larry projects his own baldness insecurities onto juror
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-specific humor. Larry's baldness paranoia creates logical profiling system.

jokes.text: I don't think I could take it, you know? And I'll bet a lot of them cheat on it too. There's probably a bad sequesterer among them.
jokes.setup: Judge explaining jury sequestration
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately assumes someone will cheat on sequestration
jokes.explanation: Sets up the eventual sequestration violation payoff. Larry's paranoia about rule-breaking proves prophetic.

jokes.text: Have you ever known love, Larry? Have you ever known the loss of a great love? Oh, I've known love and I've known loss. 2004, Yankees lost the last four games to the hated Red Sox.
jokes.setup: Cynthia asks about Larry experiencing great love and loss
jokes.punchline: Larry equates romantic heartbreak with Yankees baseball loss
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection and character moment. Larry's emotional priorities brilliantly revealed through sports reference.

jokes.text: When Messier lofted that trophy, I thought my heart was gonna burst.
jokes.setup: Continuing discussion of love and loss
jokes.punchline: Larry describes Rangers victory with romantic language
jokes.explanation: Extends the sports-as-romance theme. Larry's genuine emotion about hockey is endearing.

jokes.text: How did you do it? I think you're the first person who has ever asked me that question, Larry. So inappropriate.
jokes.setup: Larry questioning Cynthia's suicide attempt
jokes.punchline: Cynthia points out Larry's inappropriate curiosity
jokes.explanation: Larry's inappropriate curiosity creates cringe moment. Sets up the suicide method contradiction.

jokes.text: Pills, okay? What kind of pills? Pink pills. Are you sure? Yeah, I'm sure.
jokes.setup: Larry pressing for suicide attempt details
jokes.punchline: Cynthia's vague and uncertain details about her own suicide attempt
jokes.explanation: Building evidence that Cynthia's suicide story is fabricated. 'Pink pills' sounds improvised.

jokes.text: The woman tried to hang herself. Oh, she did? Well, isn't that curious. 'Cause she told me she took pills.
jokes.setup: Richard says Cynthia tried to hang herself
jokes.punchline: Larry reveals she told him a completely different method
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the suicide method setup. Larry catches Cynthia in obvious lie with Richard as unwitting accomplice.

jokes.text: What are you gonna adopt, a 40-year-old? Yeah. A doctor. A doctor? A doctor or a lawyer.
jokes.setup: Richard wants to start a family through adoption
jokes.punchline: Richard plans to adopt a fully grown professional
jokes.explanation: Absurdist escalation of adoption concept. Richard's practical approach to instant family is ridiculous.

jokes.text: Yeah. You're stealing the spotlight. By the way, why are you telling everybody that Cheryl doesn't like Mexican food?
jokes.setup: Ted protesting at Larry's trial
jokes.punchline: Larry accuses Ted of attention-seeking, then brings up trivial Cheryl complaint
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier Mexican food conflict. Larry's priorities are hilariously skewed during his trial.

jokes.text: No coasters. They want you to respect the law, but the law doesn't respect wood. You see the irony there?
jokes.setup: Larry in courthouse conference room
jokes.punchline: Larry finds irony in courthouse not protecting furniture while enforcing law
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry observation finding cosmic irony in mundane detail. Captures his worldview perfectly.

jokes.text: Larry, you fucked! Shut the fuck up. This motherfucker's good.
jokes.setup: Prosecutor's effective opening statement
jokes.punchline: Leon's blunt assessment that Larry is screwed
jokes.explanation: Leon's direct assessment cuts through legal drama. His crude honesty is refreshing.

jokes.text: Would you convict Jesus? I think we all know the answer to that. And if you wouldn't convict Jesus... Ha! ...then you can't convict Larry.
jokes.setup: Defense attorney comparing Larry to Jesus
jokes.punchline: Larry's amused reaction to being compared to Jesus
jokes.explanation: Bold legal strategy comparing Larry to Jesus. Larry's laugh shows he appreciates the absurdity.

jokes.text: That's not Joe Boccabella. That's Mocha Joe.
jokes.setup: State calls witness Joe Boccabella
jokes.punchline: Larry recognizes his old coffee shop nemesis
jokes.explanation: Callback to long-running Mocha Joe antagonist. Sets up testimony about Larry's spite store.

jokes.text: Joey Funkhouser started that fire with his big new penis.
jokes.setup: Mocha Joe accusing Larry of burning his coffee beans
jokes.punchline: Larry blames fire on Funkhouser's penis enhancement
jokes.explanation: Callback to Funkhouser's penis enhancement subplot. Absurd but fits established continuity.

jokes.text: The swan attacked me! It was kill or be killed. You're a murderer. Murderer! It was self-defense.
jokes.setup: Golf club owner accusing Larry of killing swan Yoko
jokes.punchline: Larry's increasingly desperate justification for swan murder
jokes.explanation: Callback to swan incident. Larry treating animal encounter like warfare is absurd.

jokes.text: Trump, Putin, and Larry David? That's an unusual combination.
jokes.setup: Vindman comparing Larry to Trump and Putin
jokes.punchline: Larry's bemused reaction to being grouped with dictators
jokes.explanation: Larry's understated reaction to being compared to world's worst leaders. Economical and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: My name is Journey Gunderson. My wife is in the hospital because of the dressing on your salad.
jokes.setup: Jeff trying to get Auntie Rae's recipe through deception
jokes.punchline: Jeff's elaborate fake emergency story with ridiculous name
jokes.explanation: Jeff's elaborate scheme with absurd fake identity. 'Journey Gunderson' is perfectly ridiculous.

jokes.text: Ah! Journey! The doctors need to know the specific ingredients. Journey! I'm sorry. Mr. McGunderman, is that your wife? That was Karen Gunderson. She's all catawampus.
jokes.setup: Jeff's fake hospital emergency call
jokes.punchline: Larry's over-the-top performance as dying wife plus name confusion
jokes.explanation: Larry's commitment to the bit with dramatic dying performance. Name confusion adds to chaos.

jokes.text: What the heck is wampus, Mr. Gurney Manunderson? Hang in there. Uh, Karen Gunderson, honey, me and your husband, Gurney Germerny, we gon' get you better, baby.
jokes.setup: Auntie Rae questioning the made-up word 'catawampus'
jokes.punchline: Auntie Rae's mangled versions of the fake names plus Larry's continued performance
jokes.explanation: Name confusion escalates as scheme falls apart. Auntie Rae's versions are increasingly garbled.

jokes.text: This goddamn Kramer, man, he's too much, man. Walk into your fucking house unannounced and shit. You might as well take off the fucking door and put a fucking saloon door on that bitch.
jokes.setup: Leon analyzing Kramer's behavior
jokes.punchline: Leon's practical solution of replacing door with saloon doors
jokes.explanation: Leon's unique perspective on classic Seinfeld dynamic. Saloon door solution is brilliantly practical and absurd.

jokes.text: He'll come in the courtroom in a wheelchair. And he rolls in there slow and fucking shaking and shit. Everybody's like, 'Oh, my God. That poor man.' The judge is touched. The bailiff is touched. Sketch artist is fucking bawling so bad that the sketch is blurry.
jokes.setup: Leon telling story about Horsecock Williams faking disability
jokes.punchline: Escalating description of everyone's emotional reaction, ending with blurry sketch
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation ending with perfect visual detail of sketch artist crying. Leon's storytelling is vivid and absurd.

jokes.text: Have you ever had sex with a woman in a wheelchair? 'Cause I have. Have you ever tapped some ass on crutches? You ever crutch fuck? That's like fucking an animal 'cause the crutches are an extra set of legs.
jokes.setup: Leon discussing Horsecock Williams' wheelchair hooker idea
jokes.punchline: Leon's increasingly inappropriate comparisons about disability and sex
jokes.explanation: Leon's inappropriate commentary crosses into offensive territory. Less clever than his usual observations.

jokes.text: Oh, you're here. What is going on? Honey. You came. Look, look, look, look. Honey's here.
jokes.setup: Mysterious woman in wheelchair appears at trial
jokes.punchline: Larry's over-the-top performance pretending this is his girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Larry implementing Leon's sympathy strategy with hired actress. Performance is appropriately awkward.

jokes.text: She got hit by a bus a few months ago and she was lying on the ground, I rushed over. I gave her mouth-to-mouth resuscitation. And I carried her to my car. Not that easy.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining his relationship with wheelchair woman
jokes.punchline: Larry's elaborate fake backstory gets increasingly detailed and inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Larry's commitment to the fake story with uncomfortable details. 'Not that easy' is a nice touch.

jokes.text: Somebody's gonna have to jump. What, are you fucking nuts? And so I was forced... ...to jump 45 feet. I broke both my knees, my ankles.
jokes.setup: Rachel testifying about ski lift incident with Larry
jokes.punchline: Larry's reaction to jumping suggestion, then consequences of Rachel actually jumping
jokes.explanation: Callback to classic Larry incident. His reasonable reaction contrasted with extreme consequences.

jokes.text: Larry David pissed on a portrait of Jesus Christ in my home. What? He tinkled on it. He sprayed Jesus.
jokes.setup: Witness testimony about Larry urinating on Jesus portrait
jokes.punchline: Increasingly dramatic descriptions of the urination incident
jokes.explanation: Callback to infamous Larry incident. Multiple euphemisms for urination add comedic escalation.

jokes.text: Larry David is a sad senior with long balls and mental issues.
jokes.setup: Character witness testimony against Larry
jokes.punchline: Brutally specific physical and mental assessment
jokes.explanation: 'Long balls' is hilariously specific physical detail. Brutal but economical character assassination.

jokes.text: Mommy! Mommy! That bald man's in the bathroom and there's something hard in his pants.
jokes.setup: Adult Tara recounting childhood encounter with Larry
jokes.punchline: Child's innocent description of Larry's phone creating sexual misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry innocent situation turned sexual. Child's interpretation creates perfect misunderstanding.

jokes.text: Well, his wife did. Long enough for him to use the N-word around me. He did that shit. And long enough for him to hug me and have a hard-on, an erection that he was rubbing all up against me.
jokes.setup: Auntie Rae called as character witness for Larry
jokes.punchline: Auntie Rae immediately turns against Larry with multiple accusations
jokes.explanation: Perfect betrayal by supposed character witness. Auntie Rae's accusations escalate rapidly.

jokes.text: You sons of bitches. It was you! I know it was you, Bernie Bonderman, and you, Karen Bonderman, trying to steal my recipe. Fuck you, Larry David.
jokes.setup: Auntie Rae realizes Jeff and Larry stole her recipe
jokes.punchline: Auntie Rae's explosive revelation and condemnation
jokes.explanation: Satisfying payoff to recipe theft scheme. Auntie Rae's explosion is well-earned.

jokes.text: She's walking! It's a miracle. It's just a miracle. How did she do it? I can't even believe it. Walking. Oh. Oh.
jokes.setup: Wheelchair woman stands up in anger
jokes.punchline: Larry's fake amazement at the 'miracle' recovery
jokes.explanation: Payoff to wheelchair scheme as actress breaks character. Larry's continued performance is appropriately desperate.

jokes.text: Suppose you're friends with someone who's in the circus. He knows the bearded lady very well. You go to the bearded lady's apartment. It's a normal apartment. Nothing freakish. No, nothing freakish at all.
jokes.setup: Larry posing hypothetical about dating a bearded lady
jokes.punchline: Larry's careful establishment that apartment appears normal despite occupant
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry hypothetical with careful logical construction. His specificity about 'nothing freakish' is perfect.

jokes.text: Clean-shaven bearded lady. She's quitting the circus. She's got a big crush on you. She'd love to go out with you. She's gonna start shaving. But at the party when we're there... she's got the beard on. Rabbinical? Very rabbinical.
jokes.setup: Larry's bearded lady scenario gets more specific
jokes.punchline: The beard is described as 'rabbinical' - very religious/scholarly
jokes.explanation: 'Rabbinical' is perfect Larry descriptor - intellectual and specific. Creates vivid mental image.

jokes.text: What's the worst that could happen? The worst that could happen is it's a paper napkin... ...and she wipes her mouth, and, uh, there's some-- part of it snags. What if I happen to be home at 4:30? Guess who's on the ledge.
jokes.setup: Ted asking about worst case scenario with bearded lady
jokes.punchline: Larry imagines being suicidal over seeing her five o'clock shadow
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation with perfect Larry neuroticism. The specific timing of 4:30 adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: Even I was sweating, and let me tell you something, I don't sweat, it's the craziest thing. Like I could be playing basketball in the summer, I won't sweat. You know, occasionally for some reason, I'll sweat during intercourse sometimes, which is odd.
jokes.setup: Larry testifying about how hot it was election day
jokes.punchline: Larry overshares about his sweating patterns including during sex
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry TMI moment during testimony. His confusion about sex sweating is appropriately awkward.

jokes.text: It was my 5-wood. You pried the club out of a dead man's hand? Okay. So you know when you get attached to a club, you don't wanna give it up even if it's in a coffin? Well, that was that 5-wood for me.
jokes.setup: Prosecutor asking about Larry taking golf club from coffin
jokes.punchline: Larry's passionate justification for stealing club from dead person
jokes.explanation: Larry's passionate defense of grave robbing for golf equipment. His priorities are hilariously skewed.

jokes.text: 'Devoted sister, beloved cunt.' Huh? It's a typo. Don't you see? It's a typo. The fact is... ...I've heard that she actually was a bit of a cunt. But that's still no excuse to put that in a newspaper.
jokes.setup: Prosecutor reading Larry's obituary typo
jokes.punchline: Larry's defense that it was a typo, then admission it might have been accurate
jokes.explanation: Larry making situation worse by admitting the insult might be accurate. Perfect self-sabotage.

jokes.text: So... my question is, where the fuck are the tapes at? Tapes? Larr-- The tapes. Larry won't give that shit up. The tapes. What tapes? The fuck tapes.
jokes.setup: Leon asking about Seinfeld show
jokes.punchline: Leon assumes Larry has sex tapes of all Jerry's encounters
jokes.explanation: Leon's logical extension of his 'fuck documentary' theory. His certainty about the tapes is perfect.

jokes.text: Yeah. Yeah. But they're on laser disc. Oh! Do you have a player? Fuck me, man. Yeah, sorry. No way to convert that shit to something else? Yeah. No. That's a shame. It's 13 unbelievable hours.
jokes.setup: Leon's excitement about the alleged sex tapes
jokes.punchline: Larry claims they exist but are on obsolete laser disc format
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation with absurd technological obstacle. '13 unbelievable hours' is a great specific detail.

jokes.text: There's a police report that exists stating that Larry David would not give candy to children on Halloween. I don't-- I don't think so. God, asshole! Fuck you. Thanks for nothing, asshole.
jokes.setup: Prosecutor listing Larry's past crimes
jokes.punchline: Larry refusing Halloween candy with children's profane reactions
jokes.explanation: Callback to classic Larry vs. children conflict. Kids' profanity is shocking and funny.

jokes.text: He brought a sex offender to a Passover seder. He ate a dying dog's last meal. And he fornicated... ...with a blowup doll in broad daylight.
jokes.setup: Prosecutor's litany of Larry's offenses
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd and specific crimes escalating to public sex with doll
jokes.explanation: Rapid-fire callback montage. Escalation is effective but individual items are less clever.

jokes.text: And he pretended to be an incest survivor... named Todd.
jokes.setup: Final item in prosecutor's Larry crime list
jokes.punchline: Larry's fake incest survivor persona with mundane name Todd
jokes.explanation: Callback to one of Larry's most outrageous lies. 'Todd' is perfectly mundane name for fake persona.

jokes.text: All I got left is the fucking finale. Wow. Although, I heard some terrible things about it. I heard you fucked it up.
jokes.setup: Leon finishing Seinfeld binge watch
jokes.punchline: Leon's blunt assessment that Larry ruined the finale
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about Seinfeld finale criticism with Leon's characteristic bluntness. Perfect self-deprecation.

jokes.text: We find the defendant guilty. What? Oh, come on. This is a travesty of justice. Come on.
jokes.setup: Jury delivers verdict in Larry's trial
jokes.punchline: Larry's shocked reaction to being found guilty
jokes.explanation: Standard guilty verdict reaction. Sets up the prison sentence and eventual reversal.

jokes.text: And I truly hope this time you finally learned your lesson.
jokes.setup: Judge sentencing Larry to prison
jokes.punchline: Judge invoking the 'learning lessons' theme Larry rejects
jokes.explanation: Callback to central episode theme. Ironic since Larry's 'crime' was actually learning from past mistakes.

jokes.text: That happens to me too. What do you call that? It's a pants tent. Pants tent. Yeah. I got a five-inch bunch up here.
jokes.setup: Larry in prison connecting with fellow inmate
jokes.punchline: Bonding over shared pants bunching problem with specific measurements
jokes.explanation: Larry finding common ground in prison over trivial clothing issues. Five-inch measurement adds specificity.

jokes.text: You know that Mexican restaurant near the hotel? I go there to get something to eat. I don't even like Mexican food. That's okay. I won't tell anybody. What do I care? Well, some people care. Really? Yeah.
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining how he got Larry freed
jokes.punchline: Jerry casually reveals he doesn't like Mexican food, referencing Cheryl's secret
jokes.explanation: Callback to Mexican food subplot. Jerry's casual admission contrasts with Cheryl's secrecy.

jokes.text: He's not supposed to be in a Mexican restaurant because he's under sequester. He broke his sequester? Broke his sequester. He's supposed to sequester. He's a bad sequesterer.
jokes.setup: Jerry explaining juror was eating at restaurant during sequestration
jokes.punchline: Larry's repeated use of 'sequester' as verb and 'sequesterer' as noun
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to Larry's sequestration paranoia. Wordplay on 'sequesterer' is linguistically funny.

jokes.text: This is how we should've ended the finale. Oh, my God, you're right. How did we not think of that?
jokes.setup: Larry and Jerry walking free from courthouse
jokes.punchline: Meta-commentary that this prison/freedom ending would have been better for Seinfeld
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary addressing Seinfeld finale criticism. Perfect way to end both this episode and series.

jokes.text: Well, I need it to read. I need-- Why don't you use the overhead light? Just let her keep the shade up. I'm trying to watch a movie, all I see is glare. It's like I'm watching a fucking radio right now.
jokes.setup: Airplane window shade dispute
jokes.punchline: Larry's description of watching TV with glare as 'watching a fucking radio'
jokes.explanation: 'Watching a fucking radio' is brilliantly absurd metaphor for visual interference. Classic Larry complaint escalation.

jokes.text: Go fuck yourself, Larr. We'll take a vote. Go back to fucking jail, Larry. I vote with them. It's not your window.
jokes.setup: Window shade argument continues
jokes.punchline: Everyone turning against Larry with jail references and democratic voting
jokes.explanation: Appropriate ending showing Larry learned nothing - he's immediately back to petty arguments. Jail reference callbacks.

tmdb_overview: Larry returns to Atlanta, where he gets involved in Richard's love life and reveals a secret about Cheryl.
